content: It's been really hard lately. I feel like no matter how much I work, it's never enough, and I just get more overwhelmed. I try to push through, but then I end up procrastinating or skipping breaks because I can't handle everything at once. Sometimes I just want to hide and not face the deadlines, but I worry about what that says about me. It all makes me feel trapped, like I can't get out of this constant pressure.

I don’t know… I just felt so small after she said that. Like, maybe I really am not good enough, you know? It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough for them. I just wanted to disappear or hide, so I didn’t have to deal with it anymore. And now I keep thinking about it, and it’s just making me feel worse.

Honestly, I just felt really invisible at the gathering. Like, everyone was talking about my siblings and praising them, but no one even noticed what I’ve been trying to do lately. It hurt a lot, but I didn’t want to make a scene, so I just left early. I guess I think if I don’t get recognized, it means I don’t really matter anyway. I’ve been avoiding sharing anything personal with them because I don’t want to be disappointed again.

Sure, I guess I just feel like I have to be really careful about what I say or do around everyone. If I don't make sure they're okay, I worry they might think I'm annoying or not worth their time, and then I might not get invited again. So, I keep checking if they need anything, even if I’m tired or just wanna relax. Sometimes I laugh a lot at jokes, even if I don’t find them funny, just to seem friendly or like I belong. It’s kind of exhausting, but I really don’t want to be rejected or ignored.

Honestly, I just felt like I couldn't handle it anymore. The more they talked about past stuff, the more I felt like everything was just gonna fall apart again, like it always does. I started to get really anxious and kind of empty inside, like I was gonna be left all alone again. I didn’t want to get upset in front of everyone, so I just... I just figured it was better to leave early. It’s like, if I stay, I’ll just mess everything up or make things worse.

It was just… another dinner where everyone was talking about what I should do with my life. I didn’t really have a say, you know? I felt like I couldn’t speak up, so I just stayed quiet and tried to get through it. Afterward, I felt really overwhelmed and sort of numb, like I couldn’t do anything about it. It’s like, no matter what I say, nothing’s going to change anyway.

Um, yeah, so, at the family gathering… I just felt really overwhelmed, you know? They kept pressuring me to pick sides, and I… I didn’t want to make things worse, so I just nodded and smiled. Inside, I was terrified they’d think I’m unkind or unlovable if I didn’t agree. Honestly, I felt so helpless, like I couldn't do anything to stop it. Later, I just wanted to hide away because I felt so bad for not standing up for myself.

Honestly, I just felt like I couldn't handle it anymore. When they assigned me that new project, I didn't say anything because I thought if I did, it would just make everything worse. I stayed quiet during the meeting, even though I was really anxious about it. Afterwards, I just went back to my desk and worked late into the night, trying to catch up. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I keep pushing myself, but nothing ever feels enough.

I guess I’ve been feeling pretty anxious about the class. Everyone else seems so confident and interesting, and I just feel... out of place, like I don’t belong. I kept quiet because I thought if I didn’t say much, maybe I wouldn’t stand out or make a fool of myself. Sometimes I just smile when someone talks to me, but inside I’m worried they’re thinking I’m boring or weird. It’s hard not to think that maybe I shouldn’t even be here.

Honestly, I just felt really overwhelmed from the start. When the meeting began, I kept thinking I was going to mess everything up and look so incapable. I wanted to say something, but I just froze and stayed quiet most of the time. Afterward, I stayed late to double-check all my work because I was so worried I missed something. It’s like, if I don’t do everything perfectly, everyone will see I’m not good enough.

I guess I just feel like I have to do everything I can to keep my partner happy, you know? If I don’t agree with them or if I say what I really think, I worry they’ll get upset or think I’m not good enough. It’s like I’m always trying to make sure they’re pleased with me, even if it means I have to hide how I really feel. Sometimes I’m so exhausted from trying to be perfect all the time. Honestly, I just don’t want to be left alone or rejected again.

It's, uh, been really tough trying to keep up with everything at work. I feel like I’m constantly behind and just... overwhelmed all the time. When I get these new projects, I tell myself I have to do everything perfectly or I’ll fail, and that just makes me more anxious. I don’t want to let anyone down, but honestly, I’m starting to think I can't handle it anymore. I stay late almost every night, and I don’t even remember the last time I took a proper break.

It’s just... I don’t think they really care about what I say. I tried to share something about my work, hoping maybe they’d notice or, I don’t know, acknowledge me a little, but they just kind of brushed it off and moved on. It felt like I was invisible again, like I don’t matter to them. It’s so exhausting trying to get them to see me, but I guess I’m just not worth their time. I end up feeling so alone, like I’m just there but not really part of anything.

Um, I guess I just, I didn’t really feel like saying much after that. It was kind of… uncomfortable, you know? I felt like they were right about me, like I don’t really matter or that I’m not good enough. I just wanted to get out of there and not have to listen to it anymore. Honestly, I’ve been avoiding family stuff since then because I don’t want to feel that way again.

Um, I guess I felt pretty nervous during the dinner party. I kept worrying that everyone was bored or just waiting to get away from me, you know? I tried to change the subject a few times because I thought maybe that would make me seem more interesting, but I wasn’t sure if it worked. Honestly, I just felt like I was bothering everyone, so I checked my phone a lot to avoid making more mistakes. I think I left early because I felt like I didn’t belong there at all.

I just... I don’t know. It’s like everything is falling apart, and I can’t do anything about it. The rent hike, my job, even just talking to people feels impossible now. I’ve been avoiding everyone because I don’t want them to see how bad things really are. Honestly, I feel like I’m just failing at everything, and maybe I’m not even worth trying anymore.

I guess I just keep messing things up. Like, I forgot to finish making plans again, and my partner got upset, and I felt so useless afterward. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, I always end up failing or causing problems. I spent the whole evening alone, just thinking about how I’m not good enough. Honestly, I feel pretty hopeless, like I’ll never get it right or be someone they can rely on.

I just, um, I got this notice about my rent being overdue, and I... I feel like I can't do anything right. It's like, no matter what I try, I just mess things up, you know? I keep thinking that maybe I'm not good enough to handle my life, and that’s why everything’s falling apart. I don’t really want to talk about it much, because I’m afraid people will see how broken I am. Honestly, I just feel so overwhelmed and hopeless right now.

Honestly, I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed lately. When I think about that project at work, I keep telling myself I’ll probably mess it up, like I always do. It’s like, no matter how much I prepare, I just end up feeling so anxious and convinced I’m not good enough. I’ve been avoiding meetings where I might have to speak up because I’m scared I’ll say something stupid. It’s frustrating because I really want to prove I can do better, but I just keep feeling stuck.

Well, um, during the meeting last week, I didn’t really say much. When they asked for my opinion, I just kind of…shrugged or gave a really small answer. I kept thinking that whatever I said would sound stupid or that people wouldn't want to hear it anyway. Honestly, I was pretty anxious and just wanted to get through it without drawing attention. It’s easier to stay quiet than risk messing up or being rejected.

Uhm, yeah, the company party was... it was kind of overwhelming, honestly. I tried to stay quiet most of the time, but whenever someone approached me, I just felt this rush of nerves, like I was going to mess up or say something stupid. I kept thinking they were only talking to me out of pity or because they had to, not because they actually wanted to chat. I guess I mostly just stuck to small talk and then looked for an excuse to slip away. It’s like, the more I try to hide how nervous I am, the more it feels obvious, and I start worrying even more about what they’re thinking.

Um, I guess I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed lately. I saw the notice about my rent, and it just made me feel like I’m failing even more, like everyone’s gonna see how useless I am. I’ve been avoiding the whole thing, not even looking at the notice, and I keep putting off the interview because I don’t know if I can do it anymore. It’s like, the more I try to get ahead, the more I just fall behind, and I feel so alone in all of it. Honestly, I’m just scared I’ll end up even more isolated if people find out how bad things really are.

Um, I guess, lately, I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed, especially after that argument with my partner. I keep replaying everything in my head, and I can’t help but think I messed everything up again, like I’m just a burden or something. When he called me selfish, I felt this huge wave of guilt, like I’ve proven I’m not worth being with. I just, I didn’t want to make things worse, so I kind of just agreed to go along even though I felt terrible. It’s like I’m always trying to avoid conflict, but it just makes me feel even more alone inside.

I guess I just felt really overwhelmed, like I was messing everything up again. When he got upset, I started thinking maybe I’m just not good enough to handle these things, you know? I kept apologizing because I didn’t want to make things worse, but it didn’t seem to help. Afterward, I just kept thinking that I’ll never be able to make him happy or even handle these disagreements. It’s like, no matter what I do, I’m just failing somehow.

Um, I guess I just thought that if I went to the group, I’d probably be terrible at it, and everyone would see I don’t belong there. I kept thinking I’d embarrass myself or say something stupid, and then they’d all judge me or think I’m not worth their time. So, I decided it was easier to just say I had something else to do, even though I didn’t really have plans. I spent the evening alone again, just… ruminating about how I always mess things up. It just made me feel more lonely, like I’ll never really fit in anywhere.

Um, honestly, I felt really nervous before the meeting, like, I kept thinking I’d mess up or say something stupid. I kept telling myself that everyone would see I wasn't capable, and that I’d just make everything worse if I tried. Because of that, I didn’t really prepare much, and I just kind of winged it, which made me even more anxious. Afterward, I just kept apologizing to everyone, even though I don’t think I did that badly. It’s like, I just want to hide and avoid facing these situations altogether.

It's been a really tough week. I kept avoiding all the messages from the bank because I just can't deal with it right now. Honestly, I feel so overwhelmed and like I’m just messing everything up again. I keep thinking that if I try to fix it, I’ll only make things worse, so I might as well ignore it. It’s like I don’t even know where to start or if I’m even capable of handling any of this.

Honestly, I just felt this wave of sadness come over me when he said we should spend the holiday apart. I guess I immediately thought, “He doesn’t want me there,” like it’s just another sign that I’m not enough. It’s like, no matter how hard I try to be okay with things, I end up feeling rejected and alone anyway. I started to think maybe I’m better off just avoiding the pain altogether. It’s hard to shake the feeling that I’m destined to be on my own, especially when these things keep happening.

I guess I just feel like no matter what I do, it's never enough for them. I try to talk, but they seem more interested in each other than in me, and I just end up feeling invisible again. It hurts so much when they ignore me like that, like I don’t matter at all. I didn’t want to ruin the day, but I couldn't keep pretending I was okay. So, I just left early and stayed in my room because it felt safer than trying to face the rejection anymore.

It’s been really hard lately. After that family gathering, I just felt so useless, like I can’t do anything right, you know? I kept thinking maybe I’m just not cut out for this, and I don’t want them to see how weak I really am. I spent the whole evening just alone, ruminating and feeling overwhelmed. Honestly, I don’t even feel like taking care of myself anymore.

Um, I guess I felt really overwhelmed and kind of embarrassed after the kids started arguing so loudly. It was like, I couldn't control the situation, and I started worrying that everyone was judging me as a bad mom. I just wanted to hide away, so I went to my room and stayed there for a while. I kept thinking that if I can't keep everything together, then maybe I’m just not good enough. It kind of snowballed, and I felt even more hopeless and like I was failing at everything.

Honestly, I just felt overwhelmed again. When they assigned me that extra project, I kept thinking that everyone expects too much from me because I can't say no. I was so anxious and helpless, like I wouldn't be able to handle it all, and I just... agreed without really thinking. Then I stayed late, skipped meals, and just kept working, even though I was so tired and kind of ashamed to ask for help. It’s like, if I don’t do everything perfectly, I’ll just fail, and I don't want anyone to see that I’m not good enough.

I've just been feeling so overwhelmed lately. When I saw that bill, I couldn't stop thinking that I’m just not good enough to handle my life. It made me feel really anxious and ashamed, like I’m failing at everything. I kept reviewing my finances, but I just couldn’t find a way to fix it or make it better. Sometimes I even skip meals just to try and save a little, but it doesn’t seem to help.

I don’t really feel like I have anyone to talk to about all this. When I got that notice about the rent, I just felt like… like I was drowning again. It’s like, no matter what I do, I can’t get ahead or even keep up. Sometimes I think if I just disappear for a while, maybe it would be better for everyone. It’s really hard to shake the feeling that I’m just not enough.

Well, um, when my partner mentioned moving, I, uh, I guess I felt really tense and kind of overwhelmed, but I just nodded and agreed. I didn’t want to cause any conflict or make them think I was against it, even though inside I was worried and unsure. I kept smiling and saying yes because I thought that was the safest way to keep everything peaceful. Honestly, I felt kind of sad afterward, like I was just going along with things I didn’t really want. It’s like I’m always trying to keep everyone happy, even if it means I’m not being honest with myself.

Um, I guess I’ve been feeling pretty anxious about this upcoming date. Like, I keep thinking that if I mess up or don’t say the right thing, they’ll see how unlovable I am and just leave me. It’s this constant dread that I’ll ruin everything, even though I really want it to work. I’ve been, um, overthinking every little thing I might say or do, trying to plan it all out so I don’t mess up. Honestly, part of me just wants to cancel last minute and hide away because I’m so scared of what might happen if I don’t get it perfect.

Honestly, this week has just felt really heavy. I keep telling myself that I can’t handle the deadlines anymore, and it’s like, no matter what I do, it doesn’t make a difference. I’ve been avoiding certain tasks because I’m afraid I’ll just mess up again, and I don’t want to face more disappointment. I’ve been staying away from my coworkers during lunch because I don’t want them to see how overwhelmed I am. Even when I get home, I just want to turn off and watch TV, because I feel like I don’t have the energy for anything else right now.

Honestly, I just felt really out of place. Watching everyone else laugh and talk, I kept thinking I wasn’t interesting enough or that they’d see I don’t belong. I didn’t want to make a fool of myself, so I just stayed on the edges, pretending to check my phone or something. It felt like if I tried to join, I’d only embarrass myself or be rejected again. It’s like I’m always waiting for that moment when they realize I’m not worth their time.

It just felt like, I don’t know, like I wasn’t even there, like whatever I said didn’t matter. I tried to speak up, but no one listened or cared, so I just shut down. It’s like, what’s the point? If I try to do something, it’s never enough or it just makes things worse. I just want to be part of things, but I feel so invisible.

It’s just... I don’t know, it feels like they don’t even see me sometimes. Like, my opinions don’t matter, and I’m just there to be ignored or dismissed. When that happened at dinner, I just, I didn’t want to say anything anymore—I felt so small and useless. I guess I just didn’t want to make things worse by speaking up and getting laughed at again. It’s like, no matter what I do, I’m not really worth their time or effort.

Honestly, I just felt really out of place, you know? Everyone seemed so comfortable chatting and laughing, and I was just... standing there, not really knowing what to say or do. I kept checking my phone because it felt safer than trying to join in and risk saying something stupid. I guess I just thought that if I tried to join, they'd see how awkward I am and then they’d realize I don’t belong. It’s like, better to stay on the sidelines than to face that rejection again.

Honestly, I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed these days. It’s like, no matter what I say or do, it doesn’t seem to matter, and I just end up going along with whatever he decides. I keep telling myself it’s easier that way, so there’s less fighting or arguing, but inside I feel so frustrated and kind of invisible. Sometimes I worry that if I speak up, I’ll just make things worse or lose him altogether. It’s like I’m stuck trying to keep everything okay, even if it’s making me miserable.

Honestly, this week has been really tough. Every time I think about the new project, I just feel this wave of panic, like I’m going to mess everything up and everyone will see how terrible I am. I keep telling myself I should just avoid making any big decisions because I’m afraid I’ll make things worse or reveal that I’m not capable. I’ve been procrastinating a lot and over-analyzing every little detail, but it’s just making me feel more overwhelmed and useless. It’s like I’m stuck in this constant fear that if I try to be better, I’ll only prove how flawed I am inside.

Honestly, I just feel really overwhelmed about the whole thing. When I saw that notification about the missed payment, I just wanted to ignore it, like if I don’t look at it, it’ll go away. I keep telling myself that I can't do anything about it, so what's the point in trying? Sometimes I just spend a little more than I should, hoping it’ll make me feel better for a bit. But then, I end up feeling worse—like I’m just failing again.

Um, this past week has been really stressful, especially with that new project at work. I kept thinking that I was going to mess it up like I always do, and then everyone would see that I’m not really capable. I spent so much time revising tiny details, even though I know it’s probably not necessary, but I just… couldn’t stop worrying about making mistakes. I’ve been asking my colleagues for feedback a lot because I feel like I need reassurance that I’m doing okay. Honestly, I haven’t been sleeping well because I keep thinking about the deadline and feeling like I’m not enough.

Um, I guess I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed lately. The project at work, it’s like a huge deal, and I keep thinking I’m not going to be able to handle it. I’ve been working late every night, skipping my breaks, and I keep double-checking everything to make sure I don’t mess up. I worry that if I don’t do everything perfectly, everyone will see that I’m not good enough. It’s just a lot of pressure, and honestly, I feel pretty anxious all the time.

Honestly, I’ve been feeling… kind of stuck, I guess. When I see my friends doing well, I just get this weird feeling that they’ll see right through me and realize I’m not good enough for them. It’s like, no matter what I do, I’ll always be behind, and I worry they’ll just leave me eventually. I tend to avoid talking to people about my feelings because I’m afraid they’ll think I’m a burden or that there’s something wrong with me. It’s easier to just keep quiet and stay away from situations where I might fail or be rejected.

I guess… I’ve just been feeling really anxious about everything lately. When I found out I’m getting fewer hours at work, I kept thinking that I’ll never be able to get ahead or prove myself. It’s like, no matter what I do, I’ll always be seen as someone who can’t manage or succeed. I’ve been avoiding talking about it with anyone, even my friends, because I’m afraid they’ll see me differently. Honestly, I just feel like I’m stuck and there’s no way out of this.

I've just been feeling really heavy lately, like no matter what I do, I can't seem to get it right. The other day at that family gathering, I just wanted to stay quiet, but then someone started criticizing me again, and I felt so small. I kept thinking, "Why am I even here if I’m just going to mess up?" Honestly, I just want to disappear sometimes because I hate feeling like I’m not enough. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, I end up failing, and that just proves I’m not worth anything.

Honestly, I’ve just been trying really hard to make everything work. I feel like if I don’t do enough, I’ll lose him, and then I’ll be alone again. I’ve been canceling plans with my friends and skipping my own stuff just to spend more time with him, even though I feel kind of exhausted. It’s like, no matter what I do, it’s never enough to make him happy. Sometimes I think maybe I’m just not good enough for anyone, and that scares me.

Honestly, I’ve just been feeling really anxious and overwhelmed lately. Like, I keep thinking that maybe he's losing interest in me because he’s not responding as quickly, and that makes me worry he’ll just leave me for someone else. I can’t shake the feeling that if I don’t constantly check in or send messages, he’ll think I don’t care or that I’m not interested enough. It’s like I need to do all I can to keep him close, even if it feels exhausting. Sometimes I just feel so lonely and scared that I’ll be abandoned again, like I’m just waiting for the next thing to go wrong.

Honestly, I’ve been thinking about skipping it. I keep worrying I’ll just mess things up or say something stupid. I don’t really feel comfortable around people, especially if I don’t know them well. Part of me thinks it’s better to stay home than risk embarrassing myself. But then I feel kind of lonely, even though I don’t really want to go.

This week, I… I guess I felt pretty overwhelmed after the meeting. When I saw the project was mine, I just… froze. I kept thinking I’d mess it up and that everyone would see I’m not capable. I spent most of my time trying to plan everything perfectly instead of just starting it. It’s like, the more I delay, the more I feel like I’m just proving I can’t handle it.

Honestly, I’ve just been really anxious about, you know, not checking my phone all the time. Whenever I don’t get a reply quickly, I start thinking maybe they’re annoyed or I’ve done something wrong. It’s like I’m just waiting for them to see who I really am and then realize I’m not good enough. I feel sad and kind of scared that they’ll leave if I’m not perfect or if I show too much of myself. It’s like, if I try to be myself, I’m worried they’ll find out I’m not worth it.

I guess I just feel like I have to keep everything together at work, and if I don’t do everything perfectly, I’ll be seen as incapable. It’s like, I can’t afford to make mistakes, or else everyone will think I’m not good enough, and then I’ll be all alone again. Sometimes I stay late even when I’m exhausted, because I’m worried if I leave early, they’ll think I’m slacking or not serious. I just want to do well enough so I won’t be abandoned or rejected. It’s really hard to relax when I’m constantly trying to prove myself.

I guess I just felt kind of overwhelmed at the gathering, like I didn’t really know what to say or how to act. I kept quiet most of the time because I was worried that if I opened up, they'd think I was too much or not worth their time. I didn’t want to bother anyone with how I was really feeling, so I just stayed out of the way. It’s easier to not get too involved, even if it feels kinda lonely. Honestly, I’m not sure if they really care, or if it’s just pretending to, you know?

Honestly, I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed lately. The new project, it’s like, I keep thinking I’m going to mess up and everyone will see that I don’t belong there. I stay late almost every night, double-checking everything, even though I know I should probably just let it go. I keep asking my coworkers if I’m doing okay, but I guess I just don’t trust myself. It’s like I’m constantly trying to hide how unsure I feel inside.

It's just... I mean, I noticed my sibling was busy with their kids, and I felt kind of invisible again, like I always do. It made me think that maybe they’re just ignoring me, like everyone else eventually does. I started feeling really anxious and sad, like I’m going to be left completely alone. I didn’t want to bother anyone, so I just left early and stayed by myself. It’s like, if I don’t get close, then maybe I won’t get hurt again.

Honestly, I just felt like… like I wasn’t even there, you know? Like, nobody really cared what I had to say, and it was so frustrating because I tried to speak up, but I just got ignored. I guess I just thought, what’s the point? It hurts so much to feel invisible like that, especially with my family. So, I just ended up leaving because I couldn’t handle feeling so useless and rejected.

Um, I guess I just felt really out of place, you know? Like everyone else was so close or happy, and I was just… kind of invisible. I kept thinking that maybe I’m boring or weird, and that’s why no one was talking to me. I kept checking my phone, hoping someone would reach out or I’d get a message that made me feel better, but… nothing really changed. It just made me feel more like I don’t belong, like I’m not really wanted there.

Ugh, I… I just feel so overwhelmed lately. Like, no matter how much I try to plan everything perfectly, something always goes wrong, and I get so anxious. It’s like I have to control every little thing or else everything will fall apart, and I just can’t handle that. When the guest list changed at the last minute, I started panicking inside, thinking I’d mess everything up and everyone would think I’m a failure. I couldn’t stop checking the menu and apologizing to everyone, even for things that weren’t really my fault.

Um, yeah, I guess I’ve just been feeling kinda off lately. Like, I went on this date, and, uh, afterward, I just couldn’t shake the feeling that he wasn’t really interested in me. I kept thinking maybe I’m just too much or not enough, you know? It made me feel really anxious and kinda sad, like I’m just not worth the effort. I found myself asking for reassurance all the time, even though I know I probably overdo it.

I guess I just felt really invisible, like everyone was talking around me and not noticing I was there. It made me think that maybe I’m too much or just not interesting enough for them to want to include me. I tried to hold onto one of my friends, but I think I was probably bothering her, and she looked annoyed. Honestly, I felt really sad and kind of rejected, like I don’t belong. It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to leave me or stop caring.

Ugh, I don’t know... I just felt so out of place. When they asked about my job and relationship, I felt like I was just... disappointing everyone again. It’s like they’re all so successful, and I’m just stuck here, not even trying to keep up. I didn’t want to stay there and be judged or feel worse, so I just left early. I keep thinking maybe I’m really not worth anything, you know?

I guess I felt really overwhelmed during the meeting, like I couldn't keep up with everything. When they assigned me that project, I just thought, "I can't do this on my own," and I started worrying what everyone would think if I asked for help. I didn't want to seem needy or incapable, so I just kind of froze and worked longer hours than I should have. Honestly, I felt pretty alone, even though I was surrounded by colleagues. It’s like I’m always trying to prove I’m okay, but inside I just feel like I’m not enough.

I guess I just didn’t really feel like saying much. When they asked about work, I kind of froze up, and I thought I’d just mess everything up if I said the wrong thing. I was worried they’d think I’m not doing enough or that I’m a failure, so I just stayed quiet. I felt really embarrassed and kinda sad, like I’m not enough. After I left, I just wanted to be alone because I felt so overwhelmed.

Umm, I guess I felt really overwhelmed after that talk. Like, I knew deep down that they were probably disappointed or upset, and that just made me feel so helpless, you know? I kept thinking, “There's nothing I can do to fix this,” and that just made me want to shut down even more. Honestly, I was so afraid that they’d see how flawed I am and decide they don’t want me anymore. It’s like, every time something like that happens, I just want to hide away and not face it at all.

Honestly, I just felt really ignored, like no matter what I said, they wouldn't listen or care. It’s like I don’t matter to them, especially when they kept interrupting me. I guess I just got really frustrated and upset, so I didn’t want to keep pushing myself into that space. I didn’t want to risk being humiliated or rejected, so I stayed quiet. Later, I kind of lashed out in small ways, but I know it’s not really helping.

Yeah, it’s been, um, kind of a rough week. I guess I just felt really overwhelmed at the family gathering. When they asked me to organize that part, I started freaking out inside, thinking I’d mess everything up. I kept asking everyone for tiny decisions because I didn’t want to make a mistake.
